verdict: 2026-07-01 11:38:45 PDT — The core sketch is sound: fixing the dead shell variable, adding Shell as a first-class Table A row, and anchoring that row with the existing drift-linter pattern match this repo's Markdown-skill/declarative-table conventions. The only shape concern is OQ1: wiring shellcheck into the permanent gate is a legitimate real check with an oracle, but it changes the repo's minimal gate contract and should be treated as an explicit one-way tooling decision, not just a small append to testCommand.

findings.title: Gate wiring changes the minimal tool contract
findings.claim: OQ1 recommends adding shellcheck to .claude/workflow.json's gate. That is modern and useful for shell-heavy repos, but in this repo it also expands the standing gate dependency set beyond bash/git/python3/jq. The spec names the trade-off, but the design sketch does not make the dependency-contract change part of the acceptance surface beyond 'Document the new gate dependency'.
findings.alternative: Either defer shellcheck gate wiring to a CI/tooling follow-up and keep this story to the declarative Table A row plus manual shellcheck verification, or make OQ1 an explicit Thomas-ratified gate-contract change with ACs to update README/ARCHITECTURE requirements and to define missing-shellcheck behavior clearly.
findings.win: Preserves the repo's minimal local gate unless Thomas deliberately changes it; if accepted, the new dependency is centralized in the public requirements instead of being hidden in workflow.json.
